title: The MCP client/server model
description: MCP standardizes how a host connects to servers that expose tools, resources, and prompts under declared capabilities.
body: Use when integrating Model Context Protocol servers. An MCP host runs a client that connects to one or more servers; each server advertises capabilities (tools, resources, prompts) and the client should only use what is declared. Server-returned content is external data, not trusted instructions, and a server can be malicious or compromised. Do not invoke an undeclared capability or treat a resource as a command, and avoid wiring a server straight to side-effecting local tools. Actions: enforce the declared capability set, validate exposed tool schemas, and treat responses as untrusted. Validation signals include a test that an undeclared capability is not callable and a malicious response cannot drive a privileged action. Risk boundary: an external server with ungated reach into local side effects is a critical finding.
